questions.question: Direct: Ram said, 'I am happy.' Indirect:
options: Ram said that he was happy
Ram said that he is happy
Ram says that he was happy
Ram told that he was happy
questions.correctAnswer: Ram said that he was happy

questions.question: Indirect: She asked if I had done my homework. Direct:
options: She asked 'Have you done your homework?'
She asked 'Did you do your homework?'
She said 'Do your homework.'
She asked 'Are you doing homework?'
questions.correctAnswer: She asked 'Have you done your homework?'
questions.explanation: Had done (past perfect) ← backshifted from have done (present perfect).

questions.question: Direct: He said, 'Please open the window.' Indirect:
options: He requested to open the window
He requested me to open the window
He said to open the window
He told me please open the window
questions.correctAnswer: He requested me to open the window
questions.explanation: Request (please command) → requested + person + to + infinitive.

questions.question: Direct: 'Will you help me?' she asked. Indirect:
options: She asked if I would help her
She asked if I will help her
She asked if I shall help her
She asked could I help her
questions.correctAnswer: She asked if I would help her
questions.explanation: Will → would (backshift). Yes/no question → if clause.

questions.question: Indirect: He told me that he was leaving the next day. Direct:
options: He said 'I am leaving tomorrow'
He said 'I will leave tomorrow'
He said 'I was leaving next day'
He told 'I am leaving tomorrow'
questions.explanation: Was leaving → am leaving (reverse backshift). Next day → tomorrow.

questions.question: Direct: 'Don't make noise,' the teacher said to students. Indirect:
options: The teacher told students not to make noise
The teacher said not to make noise
The teacher requested students don't make noise
The teacher asked students to not making noise
questions.correctAnswer: The teacher told students not to make noise
questions.explanation: Negative imperative → told/asked + person + not to + infinitive.

questions.question: Direct: She said, 'I will finish this work tomorrow.' Indirect:
options: She said she would finish that work the next day
She said she will finish this work tomorrow
She said she would finish this work tomorrow
She says she would finish that work next day
questions.correctAnswer: She said she would finish that work the next day
questions.explanation: Will→would, this→that, tomorrow→next day (time expression change).
